This is particularly relevant for dental or surgical tools where terminology like “digit” can be metaphorically applied; however, no actual tool described here contains four distinct finger-like projections or segments. Thus, strictly interpreting "has four digits," only instruments designed explicitly around such configurations would qualify—none meet this criterion precisely.

But reevaluating carefully: the term "digits" might be misapplied beyond anatomical context. In dental instrumentation, terms like "digital" often describe shape rather than number of fingers. So perhaps the use of "digits" here isn’t referring literally to fingernails/fingers but instead denotes segmented parts or tines—a common misuse in technical jargon.
